{
  "composes": [],
  "ir_hash": "sha256:60b3b644c9ff70cec3c377e9bfb52677081919becc01b4bbdbb7acb2c74a87fb",
  "knowledges": [
    {
      "content": "This package models Galileo's falling-body thought experiment as a comparison between two explanatory models. It does not treat vacuum falling as an observed fact inside the package.",
      "content_hash": "610c6be3894bd7c56a5048e458842ed76b267f9bddcbbd32fe57961d958e04f0",
      "declaration_index": 0,
      "exported": false,
      "format": "markdown",
      "id": "example:galileo_v0_5::context",
      "label": "context",
      "parameters": [],
      "type": "note"
    },
    {
      "content": "In the tied-body thought experiment, a heavy body and a light body are bound together and considered as one composite system.",
      "content_hash": "fafd7c2956c9b327af8b260dc12c4fcc62459b48dd4f830e66071a6eab51532c",
      "declaration_index": 1,
      "exported": false,
      "format": "markdown",
      "id": "example:galileo_v0_5::thought_experiment_setup",
      "label": "thought_experiment_setup",
      "parameters": [],
      "type": "note"
    },
    {
      "content": "The vacuum case is a counterfactual setup in which the resisting medium is absent.",
      "content_hash": "2ef798258c8579e676c040a6b7585ad642ec4d72a548bdcef6efd6e01b0e3c98",
      "declaration_index": 2,
      "exported": false,
      "format": "markdown",
      "id": "example:galileo_v0_5::vacuum_setup",
      "label": "vacuum_setup",
      "parameters": [],
      "type": "note"
    },
    {
      "content": "In air, heavy bodies are often observed to fall faster than light bodies.",
      "content_hash": "3c66ed72b484c191677f03adf37e3268be6a39711792160f8fbd2079b405b3b8",
      "declaration_index": 3,
      "exported": true,
      "format": "markdown",
      "id": "example:galileo_v0_5::daily_observation",
      "label": "daily_observation",
      "metadata": {
        "prior": 0.9,
        "prior_justification": "The everyday observation is treated as familiar empirical background, not as a new vacuum experiment.",
        "prior_records": [
          {
            "justification": "The everyday observation is treated as familiar empirical background, not as a new vacuum experiment.",
            "source_id": "user_priors",
            "value": 0.9
          }
        ],
        "prior_source_id": "user_priors"
      },
      "parameters": [],
      "type": "claim"
    },
    {
      "content": "Model A: weight itself causes heavier bodies to have greater natural falling speed.",
      "content_hash": "93c47318d5b58a1f7b8ccad72cd34c2f24c407052ae0988c37f45206f2c2cd98",
      "declaration_index": 4,
      "exported": true,
      "format": "markdown",
      "id": "example:galileo_v0_5::aristotle_model",
      "label": "aristotle_model",
      "parameters": [],
      "type": "claim"
    },
    {
      "content": "Model B: differences in falling speed in air are caused by resistance from the medium.",
      "content_hash": "26d3324a0f3ae92f210065d4f4b763a9d0699a778a0323aca10d8d9d49e8fadd",
      "declaration_index": 5,
      "exported": true,
      "format": "markdown",
      "id": "example:galileo_v0_5::medium_model",
      "label": "medium_model",
      "parameters": [],
      "type": "claim"
    },
    {
      "content": "Under Model A, heavy bodies should fall faster than light bodies in air.",
      "content_hash": "794575a22d2a9c26475375537436046601e7b854838a67b899fcf4f22a53c0ba",
      "declaration_index": 6,
      "exported": true,
      "format": "markdown",
      "id": "example:galileo_v0_5::aristotle_daily_prediction",
      "label": "aristotle_daily_prediction",
      "parameters": [],
      "type": "claim"
    },
    {
      "content": "derive warrants Under Model A, heavy bodies should fall faster than light bodies in air.",
      "content_hash": "8cf288a676da6754bf33caf90d5ecfaefb48f9a364dcce587402a783c3866d25",
      "declaration_index": 7,
      "exported": false,
      "format": "markdown",
      "id": "example:galileo_v0_5::_anon_000",
      "metadata": {
        "action_label": "example:galileo_v0_5::action::aristotle_daily_observation_path",
        "generated": true,
        "helper_kind": "implication_warrant",
        "pattern": "derivation",
        "relation": {
          "conclusion": "example:galileo_v0_5::aristotle_daily_prediction",
          "given": [
            "example:galileo_v0_5::aristotle_model"
          ],
          "type": "derive"
        },
        "review": true,
        "warrant": "If weight directly increases natural falling speed, then heavier bodies falling faster in air is expected."
      },
      "parameters": [],
      "type": "claim"
    },
    {

        "generated": true,
        "helper_kind": "implication_warrant",
        "pattern": "derivation",

    },
    {
      "content": "Under Model B, heavy bodies can fall faster than light bodies in air. and In air, heavy bodies are often observed to fall faster than light bodies. are equivalent.",

    {
      "content": "The tied composite should fall faster than the heavy body alone.",
      "content_hash": "04f681a9c6b38ff12ff847077dfa63f02713c30fbca1030b15c57a538ca5eb96",
      "declaration_index": 12,
      "exported": true,

      "id": "example:galileo_v0_5::composite_faster",
      "label": "composite_faster",
      "parameters": [],
      "type": "claim"
    },
    {
      "content": "derive warrants The tied composite should fall faster than the heavy body alone.",
      "content_hash": "f7dfbb3862994cecab8518a0f6fbf22cee4155e85745f557aa5f427d4b0fc8e6",
      "declaration_index": 13,
      "exported": false,
      "format": "markdown",
      "id": "example:galileo_v0_5::_anon_002",
      "metadata": {
        "action_label": "example:galileo_v0_5::action::aristotle_composite_faster",
        "generated": true,
        "helper_kind": "implication_warrant",
        "pattern": "derivation",
        "relation": {
          "conclusion": "example:galileo_v0_5::composite_faster",
          "given": [
            "example:galileo_v0_5::aristotle_model"
          ],
          "type": "derive"
        },
        "review": true,
        "warrant": "Under the weight-speed model, greater total weight implies greater natural falling speed. In the tied-body setup, the composite contains the heavy body plus an additional light body, so it is heavier than the heavy body alone."
      },
      "parameters": [],
      "type": "claim"
    },
    {
      "content": "The tied composite should fall slower than the heavy body alone.",
      "content_hash": "8848104441e923b2b32abc529e944578a5d6eade86de4311ae437ec87490c932",
      "declaration_index": 14,

        "warrant": "Under the same weight-speed model, the slower light body should retard the faster heavy body when the two are tied together."

      "content": "The tied composite should fall faster than the heavy body alone. and The tied composite should fall slower than the heavy body alone. contradict.",

          "reasoning": "Under the weight-speed model, greater total weight implies greater natural falling speed. In the tied-body setup, the composite contains the heavy body plus an additional light body, so it is heavier than the heavy body alone."
